Metadata.title: Prime Land For Sale | Hua Hin Soi 112 | 4 Rai 2 Ngan Chanote

hero.eyebrow: Why Hua Hin · Thailand's royal resort
hero.headline: Thailand's royal address.
hero.subtext: For over a century, Thailand's kings, aristocrats, and discerning international buyers have chosen Hua Hin. Not for parties — for permanence.

royal.label: Since the 1920s
royal.headline: Built for royalty. Chosen by the elite.
royal.body: In 1929, King Rama VII commissioned Klai Kangwon Palace — Far from Worries — as the royal summer residence on Hua Hin's shore. It was not a tourist invention. It was a deliberate royal choice: sea breeze, distance from Bangkok politics, and a setting fit for a court.
royal.body2: The royal presence shaped everything — infrastructure, security, cleanliness, atmosphere. Hua Hin has never become a party town. The palace is still actively used by the royal family today.
royal.body3: King Bhumibol Adulyadej (Rama IX) spent decades here. The palace complex and the town grew together — railways, roads, hotels and security infrastructure arrived because the monarchy was present. That legacy still defines the place.

golf.label: Golf
golf.headline: Southeast Asia's golf capital.
golf.body: 10+ championship courses within 30 minutes, including Black Mountain Golf Club and Royal Hua Hin — Thailand's oldest golf course, established 1924 by King Rama VI. Green fees from 1,500–3,500 THB. Year-round play.

location.label: Location
location.headline: 200 km from Bangkok. A world away.
location.body: 2.5 hours by car, direct train from Hua Lamphong. Hua Hin airport with regional connections. Close enough to Bangkok for business. Far enough to forget it.
location.carBody: 2.5–3 hours via Rama II highway. Door-to-door from Bangkok — the most flexible option for families and luggage.
location.trainBody: 3.5–4 hours on the scenic Gulf of Thailand route from Hua Lamphong or Bangkok Thonburi. Sleepers and 1st class A/C available.
location.busBody: About 3.5 hours from Bangkok Southern Bus Terminal — from ฿150. Simple and frequent.
location.airBody: 25 minutes from Hua Hin Airport (HHQ) for regional flights, or fly into Bangkok (Suvarnabhumi ≈ 2.5 hours by road) and continue south.
location.glanceAir: Air — HHQ 25 min from plot · BKK / DMK + drive

airport.label: Hua Hin Airport
airport.headline: Hua Hin Airport — closer than you think.
airport.body: IATA code HHQ. 25 minutes from the plot. Runway widened to 45 meters in 2026 — now Boeing 737 and Airbus A320 capable. International certification targeted for 2026. Direct connections to Kuala Lumpur via AirAsia. Regional network expanding.

train.label: Bangkok by train
train.headline: Bangkok to Hua Hin — 3.5 hours by train.
train.body: Direct train from Bangkok Hua Lamphong station. 3.5–4 hours, scenic Gulf of Thailand route. Multiple departures daily. 1st class air-conditioned carriages available from ฿200–800.
train.body2: Hua Hin Railway Station — built in 1926 in Victorian style, one of the most beautiful stations in Thailand. The Royal Pavilion (Phra Mongkut Klao) was built for the royal family's arrivals.
train.body3: Prefer the bus? Bangkok to Hua Hin takes about 3.5 hours from the Southern Bus Terminal, from ฿150.

quiet.label: Why not Phuket
quiet.headline: Quiet by design.
quiet.intro: Phuket has parties. Pattaya has nightlife. Hua Hin has golf, seafood, jazz bars, and a beach that was good enough for three generations of Thai kings.
quiet.body: Phuket has transformed into a mass tourism destination — traffic, overdevelopment, noise, and a nominee land crisis. Hua Hin remained deliberate. The royal presence kept it that way. The expat community here is families, retirees, and long-term residents — people who chose quality over chaos.
quiet.phuket5: Heavy nominee / company land risk in tourist zones
quiet.huaHin1: Royal town — calm by design since the 1920s
quiet.huaHin2: Golf, seafood, jazz — not full-moon parties
quiet.huaHin3: Families and retirees, not party weekenders
quiet.huaHin4: Genuine expat community with deep roots
quiet.huaHin5: Royal presence shaped security and infrastructure
quiet.nomineeNote: Tourist hotspots also carry higher enforcement attention on nominee land structures in 2026.

expats.label: The community
expats.headline: 71 nationalities. One community.
expats.body: Over 5,000 expats call Hua Hin home, representing 71 nationalities. Scandinavians have been here since the 1990s — drawn by the golf, the climate, and the calm. Europeans, Australians, Americans, and Thais from Bangkok share a genuinely international lifestyle.
expats.body2: Sunday markets, live jazz every night, organic cafes, international schools, Bangkok Hospital 15 minutes away. It is not a retirement ghetto — it is a life.

investment.label: Investment
investment.headline: Why values are rising.
investment.body: Hua Hin's town plan is expanding from 87 to 327 km² — Thap Tai is included. The airport is being upgraded to international standard. Pool villas in the Soi 112 corridor sell for ฿15M–25M. This is why Botanica, La Felice and Mali Prestige all chose this corridor.
investment.point1Body: Hua Hin's municipal plan is expanding from 87 to 327 km² — Thap Tai and the Soi 112 corridor are inside the growth zone.
investment.point2Body: Hua Hin Airport expansion toward international capability (2026) shortens the journey for European and regional buyers.
investment.point3Body: Finished pool villas in this corridor trade around ฿15M–25M — land values trend up as infrastructure and prestige projects land.
investment.point4Body: This is why Botanica, La Felice and Mali Prestige chose Soi 112 — the corridor already carries luxury brand gravity.
investment.caption: This is why Botanica, La Felice and Mali Prestige chose Soi 112.

retirees.title: Retiring in Hua Hin — The American's Guide
retirees.introBody: Over 50,000 Americans live in Thailand. A growing number chose Hua Hin — quiet, royal, 2.5 hours from Bangkok, with JCI-accredited hospitals and golf year-round. Cost of living 50–60% below the US.
retirees.visaOABody: 1 year, renewable. Must be 50+. Requires proof of income ($2,000/month) or savings ($25,000 in Thai bank).
retirees.visaOXBody: Up to 10 years. Higher financial requirements.
retirees.visaLTRBody: New since 2022, up to 10 years, expanded property rights.
retirees.visaLTRLink: → Official LTR Visa portal (Board of Investment)
retirees.visaDisclaimer: Always consult a licensed Thai immigration lawyer. Visa rules change — verify current requirements independently.
retirees.visaEmbassyPrefix: US Embassy Bangkok also maintains a helpful guide for Americans:
retirees.costCheaperLabel: cheaper than equivalent US coastal town
retirees.healthBody: Bangkok Hospital Hua Hin is JCI-accredited with English-speaking doctors. San Paolo Hospital nearby. Many American retirees cite healthcare as the #1 reason they chose Hua Hin over other Thai cities.
retirees.hospitalCta: More about hospitals near the plot →
retirees.lifestyle6: 4 minutes: 7-Eleven. 10 minutes: Bluport Mall. 15 minutes: Bangkok Hospital.
retirees.ctaTitle: Build your retirement home on Soi 112.
retirees.ctaBody: 4 Rai 2 Ngan of flat Chanote land in Hua Hin's most prestigious corridor. Build your villa exactly as you want it. From $63,000/rai. Partial sale from 1 rai available.

europeanRetirees.heroAlt: Hua Hin golden hour — calm European retirement lifestyle
europeanRetirees.title: Retiring in Hua Hin — The European's Guide
europeanRetirees.heroSubtext: Hua Hin has been Europe's favourite Thailand address since the 1990s. Over 58% of Hua Hin expats are Western European. Here is everything you need to know.
europeanRetirees.intro1: Largest Scandinavian expat community in Thailand outside Bangkok
europeanRetirees.intro2: Dutch, German, British and Scandinavian communities well established
europeanRetirees.intro3: Royal town — calm, safe, no mass party tourism
europeanRetirees.intro4: Golf year-round — hugely popular with European retirees
europeanRetirees.intro5: 60–70% cheaper than an equivalent lifestyle in the Netherlands, Germany or Scandinavia
europeanRetirees.intro6: Direct flights from Amsterdam (KLM), Frankfurt (Lufthansa), Copenhagen/Stockholm via connecting
europeanRetirees.visaOABody: 1 year, renewable annually. Must be 50+. Financial requirement: 800,000 THB in a Thai bank (≈ €21,000) OR 65,000 THB/month income (≈ €1,700/month). Health insurance required.
europeanRetirees.visaOXBody: Up to 10 years (2×5). Higher financial requirements. Less paperwork annually.
europeanRetirees.visaPrivilegeBody: 5–20 years. From ฿600,000 (≈ €16,000) one-time fee. VIP airport service, no financial proof required.
europeanRetirees.visaLTRBody: New since 2022, up to 10 years. For wealthy global citizens, retirees and remote workers. Expanded property rights.
europeanRetirees.visaDisclaimer: Visa rules change regularly. Always verify current requirements at immigration.go.th or consult a licensed Thai immigration lawyer.
europeanRetirees.pensionTitle: Your European pension works in Thailand
europeanRetirees.pensionNlBody: Payable worldwide including Thailand — check SVB for current rules.
europeanRetirees.pensionDeBody: Payable in Thailand — check DRV for details.
europeanRetirees.pensionUkBody: Payable in Thailand but FROZEN (no annual increases) — important caveat for British retirees.
europeanRetirees.pensionTaxNote: Thailand has double taxation treaties with the Netherlands, Germany, Sweden, Denmark and the UK. Consult a tax advisor before moving.
europeanRetirees.costCompare3: German private healthcare: €400+/month → Bangkok Hospital cover: €50–120/month
europeanRetirees.healthBody: Bangkok Hospital Hua Hin is JCI-accredited with English-speaking doctors and European staff. San Paolo Hospital nearby offers international-standard care.
europeanRetirees.healthInsurance: Many European expats use BUPA Thailand or AXA International
europeanRetirees.healthEhic: European Health Insurance Card (EHIC): not valid in Thailand — get private insurance before moving
europeanRetirees.healthDental: Dental care: €30–60 per visit (vs €100–300+ in Europe)
europeanRetirees.healthPharmacy: Pharmacy: full range of European medications available
europeanRetirees.flightsTitle: Europe to Hua Hin — your options
europeanRetirees.flightKlm: KLM: Amsterdam (AMS) → Bangkok (BKK) daily direct — ~11 hours
europeanRetirees.flightThai: Thai Airways: multiple European cities → Bangkok
europeanRetirees.flightTrain: Train: 3.5–4 hours from Hua Lamphong, from ฿200
europeanRetirees.flightBus: Bus: 3.5 hours from Southern Terminal, from ฿150
europeanRetirees.flightHhq: Hua Hin Airport (HHQ): 25 min from the plot, expanding to international 2026
europeanRetirees.communityScandinavian: Scandinavians: largest European group, here since the 1990s — Swedish/Norwegian/Danish expat clubs active
europeanRetirees.communityDutch: Dutch: strong community, Dutch restaurants, Nederlandse gemeenschap
europeanRetirees.communityBritish: British: English everywhere — menus, hospitals, estate agents
europeanRetirees.communitySocial: Active social scene: golf clubs, expat associations, markets, jazz bars
europeanRetirees.nomineeTitle: The 2026 nominee crackdown — what European buyers need to know
europeanRetirees.nominee1: Thailand is actively prosecuting illegal nominee land structures in 2026
europeanRetirees.nominee2: 852 companies prosecuted · THB 15.1B in damages identified
europeanRetirees.nominee3: Safe options: registered 30-year lease, condo freehold
europeanRetirees.nominee4: This plot: clean Chanote title, Thai seller, no nominee structure
europeanRetirees.ctaTitle: Build your European dream home on Soi 112.
europeanRetirees.ctaBody: 4 Rai 2 Ngan flat Chanote land in Hua Hin's most prestigious corridor. Build your villa exactly as you want it. From ฿2,300,000/rai · ≈ €58,000/rai. Total ≈ €270,000 for the full plot. Partial sale from 1 rai available.

golf.heroAlt: Majestic Creek Country Club golf course Hua Hin
golf.title: Golf — minutes from the plot
golf.subtitle: Majestic Creek Country Club & Hua Hin's golf corridor
golf.figCaption: Majestic Creek Country Club · 5 min from plot
golf.majesticBody: Prestigious country club with direct access via Route 2004 — one of the closest championship courses to the plot, making Soi 112 a genuine golf address rather than a compromise location.
golf.majesticFeatures: 27-hole course · wide fairways · caddy service · restaurant on site
golf.courseBanyanNote: ≈ 20 min · Consistently rated among Asia's top courses
golf.coursePineappleNote: ≈ 20 min · Dramatic mountain backdrop, exceptional conditioning
golf.corridorBody: Soi 112 is known locally as Hua Hin's golf corridor — a stretch where premium residential development and world-class golf coexist. For buyers who golf regularly, living here means tee times without the commute.

size.title: 4 Rai 2 Ngan — what does that mean?
size.subtitle: Understanding the Thai land measurement system
size.systemBody: Thailand uses its own traditional land measurement system. The three units you need to know are Rai, Ngan and Tarang Wa — they work together like metres and centimetres.
size.calcFootballValue: the size of a football pitch
size.buildTitle: 🏡 What can you build on 4 Rai 2 Ngan?
size.build1: • A generous private villa with large pool and tropical garden — on just 1 Rai
size.build2: • 3–4 separate building plots with room to spare
size.build3: • A boutique villa development of 4–5 homes
size.build4: • A private estate with main house, guest villa and extensive grounds
size.buildNote: For context: most plots available on Soi 112 are 1–2 Rai. At 4 Rai 2 Ngan, this plot is exceptionally large for the area.

americanRetirees.heroAlt: Relaxed Hua Hin lifestyle for American retirees
americanRetirees.title: The retirement you actually imagined.
americanRetirees.heroSubtext: Over 50,000 Americans live in Thailand. A growing number chose Hua Hin — quiet, royal, 2.5 hours from Bangkok, golf year-round, JCI-accredited hospitals. Cost of living 50–60% below the US.
americanRetirees.whyAlt: Hua Hin neighbourhood — royal resort character
americanRetirees.whyLead: Hua Hin is Thailand's royal resort town — not a party destination. The King's palace is here. That means better infrastructure, security and cleanliness than anywhere else on the Gulf coast.
americanRetirees.why1: Forbes calls Thailand one of the world's top retirement destinations for Americans
americanRetirees.why2: 50,000+ Americans live in Thailand — Hua Hin is a top choice for retirees
americanRetirees.why3: Golf: 10+ courses, green fees from $45 — year-round
americanRetirees.why4: Healthcare: JCI-accredited hospitals with English-speaking doctors at a fraction of US costs
americanRetirees.why5: No Thai state income tax on foreign-sourced income in many cases (consult a tax advisor)
americanRetirees.why6: Quiet, international community — families and retirees, not full-moon chaos
americanRetirees.visaTitle: Your path to legal long-term stay
americanRetirees.visaOABody: 1 year, renewable. Must be 50+. Proof of income: $2,000/month OR savings: $25,000 in a Thai bank (800,000 THB). Health insurance required.
americanRetirees.visaPrivilegeBody: 5–20 years. From ฿600,000 one-time (≈ $17,000). VIP airport service, no financial proof needed.
americanRetirees.visaLTRBody: New since 2022, up to 10 years. For retirees with $80,000/year income or $250,000 in assets. Expanded property rights.
americanRetirees.visaWarning: US Embassy Bangkok has a helpful retirement resource page. Always verify current requirements before applying.
americanRetirees.moneyTitle: Social Security works here. Taxes need attention.
americanRetirees.money1: US Social Security: payable worldwide — you receive it in Thailand via direct deposit
americanRetirees.money2: Medicare: does NOT cover you outside the US — get private insurance
americanRetirees.money3: US taxes: Americans are taxed on worldwide income regardless of residence — consult a US expat tax specialist
americanRetirees.money4: Thailand–US tax treaty: exists but limited — get advice
americanRetirees.money5: Good news: Thailand does not tax foreign-sourced income brought in after the year it was earned (2024 rule — verify with a tax advisor)
americanRetirees.costTitle: Live the life you want. For a lot less.
americanRetirees.costCheaperLabel: cheaper than equivalent US coastal town
americanRetirees.healthBody: Bangkok Hospital Hua Hin is JCI-accredited — the same standard as major US hospitals — with English-speaking doctors, many US/UK trained. No insurance pre-authorization maze. San Paolo Hospital is a nearby alternative.
americanRetirees.health1: Average doctor visit: $30–60 · Major surgery often 30–50% of US costs
americanRetirees.health3: Recommended insurers: Cigna Global, AXA International, BUPA Thailand
americanRetirees.health4: Medicare does not travel with you — private cover is essential
americanRetirees.flightLax: Los Angeles (LAX) → Bangkok (BKK): 17–20 hours (Thai Airways, EVA Air, Korean Air via Seoul)
americanRetirees.flightHubs: Best connections via: Seoul (ICN), Tokyo (NRT), Taipei (TPE), Hong Kong (HKG)
americanRetirees.flightTrain: Train: 3.5 hours from Hua Lamphong, from $6
americanRetirees.flightBus: Bus: 3.5 hours from Southern Terminal, from $4
americanRetirees.flightHhq: Hua Hin Airport (HHQ): 25 min from the plot — expanding to international 2026
americanRetirees.flightsTip: Many American retirees fly to Bangkok 2–3 times a year for visa runs or US visits. Round trip Bangkok–US: $600–1,200 depending on airline and timing.
americanRetirees.communityTitle: Americans in Hua Hin — you're not alone.
americanRetirees.community3: Active Facebook groups: "Americans in Hua Hin", "Expats in Hua Hin"
americanRetirees.community4: Golf buddies easy to find — 10+ courses, many English-speaking groups
americanRetirees.community5: International schools for families · Rotary Club and expat associations active
americanRetirees.community6: 71 nationalities — genuinely international without feeling isolated
americanRetirees.nomineeTitle: The 2026 nominee crackdown — what American buyers need to know
americanRetirees.nominee1: Thailand is actively prosecuting illegal nominee land structures in 2026
americanRetirees.nominee2: 852 companies prosecuted · THB 15.1B in damages identified
americanRetirees.nominee3: Safe options: registered 30-year lease, condo freehold
americanRetirees.nominee4: This plot: clean Chanote title, Thai seller, no nominee structure
americanRetirees.ctaAlt: Hua Hin golden hour — your retirement plot
americanRetirees.ctaTitle: Build your American dream home. In paradise.
americanRetirees.ctaBody: 4 Rai 2 Ngan · 1.78 acres · Flat Chanote land. From $63,000/rai · Total ≈ $285,000. Partial sale from 1 rai ≈ $63,000+. Pool villas in this corridor sell for $400K–$700K — your land cost stays under $300K total.
